evals.prompt: I want to run DSSAT for maize in a small area of Honduras. Bounding box is [-87.5, 14.2, -87.2, 14.5], year 2021. I already have the weather cube at D:/cubes/climate_hnd_2021.nc and the soil cube at D:/cubes/soil_hnd.nc. The output should go to D:/results/yield_hnd.nc. Use cultivar IB1072 with planting date May 15 2021.
evals.expected_output: Skill asks to confirm plan, generates with_cubes YAML config, shows validate command, shows run command with ag-cube-cm CLI.

assertions.name: confirms_before_writing
assertions.description: Skill shows a plan and asks for confirmation before generating files

evals.prompt: I need to simulate wheat yield for a region in Ethiopia (bbox: [37.0, 8.0, 38.0, 9.0]) from 2018 to 2020. I don't have any datacubes yet. Use spring wheat and planting date October 1. Download climate from CHIRPS and CHIRTS. For solar radiation, I don't have a CDS key so use NASA POWER instead. Output everything to E:/ethiopia_wheat.
evals.expected_output: Skill asks to confirm, generates full_pipeline YAML config with rsds source nasa_power, correct bbox/dates, IB1015 wheat cultivar (or clarifies cultivar), output to E:/ethiopia_wheat.

assertions.name: nasa_power_for_rsds
assertions.description: Config uses nasa_power source for rsds variable

assertions.name: no_spaces_in_working_path
assertions.description: working_path in config contains no spaces

evals.prompt: My DSSAT simulation finished but I'm confused by the output. It says: pixels_ok=12, pixels_skipped=45, pixels_failed=0, mean HWAM=6200 kg/ha. Why are so many pixels skipped? Is the result reliable?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains edge-pixel NaN issue from coarser AgERA5/SoilGrids grids, explains that skipped pixels are expected at grid edges and are not failures, provides code to inspect HWAM spatially and assess coverage, and confirms result is reliable given pixels_failed=0.

assertions.name: explains_edge_nan_cause
assertions.description: Response explains that skipped pixels come from coarser AgERA5 or SoilGrids grids not fully covering the CHIRPS extent

assertions.name: distinguishes_skipped_vs_failed
assertions.description: Response clearly differentiates skipped (NaN data, expected) from failed (DSSAT error, concerning)

assertions.name: addresses_reliability
assertions.description: Response confirms result is reliable since pixels_failed=0

assertions.name: provides_inspection_code
assertions.description: Response includes Python/xarray code to inspect HWAM spatial coverage
